speaker: MATT
raw_text: Being relatively before noon at this point in time, you're heading northward along the Amber Road. The well-packed dirt of the ten-foot roadway gives you a clear path towards your next destination. The gentle creak of the cart mingles with the clomping of the horse hooves as you push forward. The midday air is warmer than you expected. The sky's common gray presence is broken into smaller cloud patches, the victorious blue peeking through. You guys continue for a few hours, talking amongst yourselves a bit. If there are any questions you wish to ask each other, now is the time.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: We chased after it, right?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You do. You chase after it to keep up.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: We don't just lose it.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: A few hours into travel along the road, you catch ahead the sight of another caravan heading southward in your direction. They begin to crest over one of the hills. As you look forward, too, the central portion of the Marrow Valley isn't just a smooth, constant grade. It's a series of rolling hills and high grasses and wheats and grains, and a lot of wild weeds that have taken over the areas between bastions of civilization.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Oh!

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I've got some things I was working on. Like this.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: You juggling those big motherfucking swords?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You watch as he balances one on the edge of the hand and then tosses it up and catches it as it falls, and takes the other one and glides it across, spins it up, catches it on his chin. On his nose; he makes it hop around. It's pretty decent.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Little roll.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Can you swallow it?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Make an acrobatics check if you want to try.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Well, it's a curved sword. So not the curved ones. But I could probably do a straight sword. I'm not going to swallow a curved sword! That would be very complicated to pull off.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: If anybody can do it, it's you.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: That tiefling anatomy is very strange, you know.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I'm flexible, but not that flexible. I'm pretty flexible. Yeah, I never nailed anything down for a really good act.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Yes. I would like you both, currently about an hour's journey past that intersection with the other caravan, to make a perception check.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Always go for a sure thing.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: You know how we're pretty sure that everyone on this road's going to kill us? Yeah, everyone else thinks that, too.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Fair enough.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: But if you need me to teach you how to steal, you can tag along sometime.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I might take you up on that, yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: All right.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Interesting.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Continuing on your path, I want to ask which two of you are taking point in keeping an eye on the road and the landscape around you as you progress?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: (fake cough) Beau.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, I'll take watch.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Oh, sorry.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay. Beau and who else?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: So generous of you.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: You want to do it?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I was going to say, yeah. I'll take a round. I'm used to keeping an eye out on the road.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: So the two of you are taking point up until you guys decide to change it when it comes to travel along the road.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: It's just general perception?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: I'd say total right now, you've been probably on the road for about four hours.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Ooh! I bet someone's going to try to hijack our shit on the road just like we were talking about then.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Not too bad. 18.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yeah! Smash.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: 18 and eight. Actually, both of you don't really notice anything.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Oh. Take that back. Take it back.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Both of you keeping an eye out, the hills continue to roll. You see patches of trees and small wooded areas. Grass field is tall but it's beautiful and the light breeze that blows through, you can see the waves of wind as they come across. You can hear the faint sound of it moving the fields across.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Is there a tree that looks climbable that I could get a vantage point?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: There is. It's maybe about 600 feet off the road, but you can try and head over there if you want to.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Plus we've been traveling for what? About hour and a half, two hours now?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Eventually, you all gather yourselves again back at the wagon, cart it back onto the road, and head northward. Another few hours pass before the sun begins to set. As the sky grows darker, the clouds from the north-northeast begin to filter in with a colder wind. You get the sense that the warm day that you had is coming to a close and who knows how long the current cold weather will keep. The light begins to fade, darker and darker, until you get the sense that you have about 30 minutes before this field goes pitch black.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay, cool. It's a nice view. (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: It's impossible to believe him.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Is there anything carved in the branches, in the tree trunk?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Make an investigation check.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: The treant eats you.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Shh, don't spoil it!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: No carvings of names or letters, but you do see a few knots in the tree that have currently been carved out and made a home to a number of woodland creatures.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I've never climbed a tree before.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Really?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah. This is really pretty.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: What do you think lives in that hole?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Probably very small unicorns.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Have you ever seen an unicorn?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Yes, I've seen many. The little ones can be as small as hamsters.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Pocket unicorns?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Where do you find such things?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: All over. You just have to know where to look.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: And believe.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: She's starting to believe.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: What is it like being in your head?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: It's pretty great!

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Eventually, you all gather yourselves again back at the wagon, cart it back onto the road, and head northward. Another few hours pass before the sun begins to set. As the sky grows darker, the clouds from the north-northeast begin to filter in with a colder wind. You get the sense that the warm day that you had is coming to a close and who knows how long the current cold weather will keep. The light begins to fade, darker and darker, until you get the sense that you have about 30 minutes before this field goes pitch black.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay, cool. It's a nice view. (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: It's impossible to believe him.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Is there anything carved in the branches, in the tree trunk?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Make an investigation check.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: The treant eats you.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Shh, don't spoil it!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: No carvings of names or letters, but you do see a few knots in the tree that have currently been carved out and made a home to a number of woodland creatures.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I've never climbed a tree before.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Really?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah. This is really pretty.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: What do you think lives in that hole?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Probably very small unicorns.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Have you ever seen an unicorn?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Yes, I've seen many. The little ones can be as small as hamsters.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Pocket unicorns?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Where do you find such things?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: All over. You just have to know where to look.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: And believe.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: She's starting to believe.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: What is it like being in your head?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: It's pretty great!

speaker: MATT
raw_text: It takes you a little bit. You spend about 45 minutes in pitch dark keeping an eye in that direction before you come upon the tree. You can faintly see the outline of the tree against the faint bit of light making it through the cloud cover. You guys stop there, get the horse a little bit of water and feed, and the site's yours.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: How cold is it out?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You can see your breath.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh, that's pretty cold.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Not super thick, but when you exhale you see a bit of the breath before you.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Should we pull off on the side of the road and get a campfire going?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I'd say let's get a bit off the road, if possible.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Does it look like there's any trees or any base of mountains or any place that would be a good sheltered campsite?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: There are no mountains anywhere in the vicinity. You're in the center of the rolling hills. There is a cluster of trees you could go to that's about 300 feet or so off the road. It's three trees, so it's not a lot of cover, but unfortunately you're in the middle of a valley.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: It'll do.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Can we get the horse over to them?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Eventually you get a fire going. It's a little beacon of light here in the darkness, orange-ish flickering against the interior of the three trees as you guys are situated there. You figure you've made it a little under halfway to Alfield, which is the small city on the way to Zadash. It'll take you a little over a day's travel, or if you push it ahead you could probably make it in a day, but you can run the risk of getting tired. But for resting this evening, who wants to take watch?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Shall we collect some wood and start a fire?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Sounds good.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Who's starting the fire?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Anybody want to come with me to find some kindling?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I have an adventurer's kit. Does that have a tinder in it?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You can do that.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Shall we?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Sure.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: All right. Go look for some dead-ish branches.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I'm a little-- you be my eyes. It's super dark.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Maybe you shouldn't go.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: You know what, I'll start digging a little pit.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Now, hold on. I pull a rag out, and I (poof) one of the Dancing Lights, and make that hooded lantern again so that you can see.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: With that light, it's easy enough to find some small kindling. There's not a whole lot in the vicinity, mainly being that it's an open field. But you find a few pieces of fallen tree and general dead plant life in the vicinity that can get something going.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: We can chop it up.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: All right, natural one. About ten minutes--

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Oh, nice.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I get my little bouquet of wildflowers and I give it to Nott.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Oh, that's very nice of you.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Well, you said all girls like flowers.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: We do. That's very nice of you, thank you. What did I do to deserve this?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I don't know, you are my friend.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: A single tear pours down a porcelain cheek.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I'll take first watch.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Everybody hears a little (ting) as I hit a little bell that I have that's part of it. And that's it.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I'll sit up with you, Jester, and take first watch, 1B.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I like it.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right. We'll say for the purposes of expediency on this, I'll take three perception rolls from three different people that are keeping watch throughout the evening. So with the setup of your spell, you go to rest. Jester, you first.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh, god damn it!

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: No, it's pretty good.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, one. So pretty good.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: About ten minutes into your watch, the inspiration hits for you to start drawing interesting faces and weird trees. You start sketching--

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Oh, that's very nice of you.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Well, you said all girls like flowers.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: We do. That's very nice of you, thank you. What did I do to deserve this?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I don't know, you are my friend.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: A single tear pours down a porcelain cheek.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I'll take first watch.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Everybody hears a little (ting) as I hit a little bell that I have that's part of it. And that's it.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I'll sit up with you, Jester, and take first watch, 1B.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I like it.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right. We'll say for the purposes of expediency on this, I'll take three perception rolls from three different people that are keeping watch throughout the evening. So with the setup of your spell, you go to rest. Jester, you first.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh, god damn it!

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: No, it's pretty good.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, one. So pretty good.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right, natural one. About ten minutes--

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Sorry, guys.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: For one round.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: How far am I from the camp?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You're about, maybe, 80 to 100 feet from it.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Cool. I'm going to pull out a piece of copper wire and, remembering the motions that Caleb taught me, I'm going to twirl it around in my hand and hold it up to my mouth and whisper a Message just to Caleb.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Oh shit, what?!

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I'm going to say  it's three buffalo, or buffalo-type objects. Creatures. What do you want me to do? (fwoosh)

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I'm so proud! I don't know that spell, so I cannot talk back.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Yes, you can!

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: What? Who said that? If you can can hear me-- I don't think you can-- come back with the group.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: All right.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Can you hear him?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Is that how Message works?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Yes, I can hear you.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: That is awesome!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: The whispers continue back and forth.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: It's just one back and forth. I can talk to him, he can reply once. That's it.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: While Molly and Jester continue to enjoy their continued long rest, you all interrupt it to get up, and head in the direction of these creatures. I would like you all to make a stealth check, please.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Hey, what the fuck did you wake us up for, anyway?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: (laughs nervously) Got you.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: That's a joke?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: No. I thought we were all going to die, but it was just-- what was it?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Three large beasts of burden. Wait, we could use them to pull the cart!

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yeah! Where are they?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: They went that way! In the dark!

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: You sure they were just cows? Or donkeys? Or buffalo-type things?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I don't know! I don't know what they were, but they looked big and able to pull the cart.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Let's go look at one.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: You want to go cow-tipping, don't you?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Fuck yeah! Take me cow-tipping, son! Woo!

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Echoes in the field. (echoing sounds)

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: What happened?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I don't know. Back in college.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Are we going to go after them or are we going to go back to sleep?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, let's go look at one.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Well, shit. I guess we're going to look at a buffalo.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Let's go!

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I'm still sleeping.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Fjord, as darkness takes your mind, dreamless like most, you await the emptiness of sleep to carry you to the day. Your sense of body fades, but your mind and the dark linger. Minutes, hours, days. Time loses real sense and meaning, but you're conscious for it and that is almost maddening, in that split second or endless stretch of experience that you're currently unable to express. As the time passes, the air grows colder around you. The faint shimmer of shifting midnight blue, muted and distant, now showing endless depth before you, rippling. You begin to flail about, your senses returning with the dropping temperature, but there is resistance to your limbs as you push in the space around you. You're sluggish. There's force. It's not air, it's water. You suddenly feel the burning pain in your lungs as your breath fights to escape you, but you hold tight. You seize and swim in desperation, choosing any direction, but the shimmering blue is directionless. There is no up, there is no down. It's just depth. Movement. The universal shadows dance as one, right to left. With a speed that pulls you along with the current, you feel yourself being jostled. As it shifts again to the left to right, around behind you, you find yourself toppling, head over end, eventually coming to rest again. You spiral over and over again, the darkness swallowing you, tightening around you as this dance, this shape, this endless coil, this spiral around you. As the current tugs at you, battering you, you cannot fight any more as the increased power of the current pulls on your form. You cough, and the freezing waters rush into your body, the briny taste of salt water stinging your insides. Your wincing eyes open with the pain that clutches the interior of your chest, only to see a gargantuan mass moving through the water around you, like a massive noose closing in. The pain in your chest vanishes. As your breathing normalizes-- thicker than air, but breathable all the same-- it feels strange yet weirdly familiar. In that moment, yellow light bursts before you in the shape of an enormous yellow eye. An impossibly low tone rumbles through the waters around you, shaking you to your core, like an organic war horn the size of a canyon. The eye narrows as the tone grows stronger. A thought enters your mind, a jumble of emotions that your brain attempts to make sense of until a word congeals into the center of your consciousness: "Watching." The eye just looks at you, waiting expectantly.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Sorry.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: That was fun, let's go.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Back to sleep.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Silently ruffle Nott's hair on the way back to camp.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Stop it.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: (singing) Who's got the third watch?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I'll take it.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Turd watch.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right. Molly has the third watch.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Turd watch.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Perception check, please.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh no!

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Six.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay. As you keep an eye out, everyone else sleeps throughout the evening. Eventually, you nod off, and fall asleep with the rest of them. I would like to ask all of you to leave the table for a moment, except for Fjord.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: For me?!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: It's your turn now!

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Why? (shouting) Why didn't you roll a higher perception?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I literally don't know. That's actually the problem.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Just don't get a lich phylactery installed in you again.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Travis, it's been nice playing with you.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Oh god. Oh no!

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I thoroughly enjoyed your accent.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Oh shit!

speaker: MATT
raw_text: "Patience." The eye closes, and the light is gone. You feel the darkness and the cold completely suffuse your entire world as the panic and solitude, the true quiet of your unconsciousness, begins to take hold. At that moment, you gasp for breath, and your eyes open, coughing and sputtering in the morning air. If we could have everybody else come back to the table, please! Call them back in.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: (English accent) What are you?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Watching."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Watching me?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: The eye opens a bit and leans closer towards you. "Potential."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: My potential? What are you asking of me?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Learn."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Learn. What would you have me learn?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Learn. Grow. Provoke. Consume."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Learn, grow, provoke, consume. I don't understand.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Reward."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: A reward for this? I'm listening.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: As the rest of you have already come to consciousness in the morning air, begun to situate your belongings and put out the embers of the fire that lasted-- pardon-- throughout the night. Your morning belches getting out. Fjord has slept in, and has been ever so still in his sleep. Suddenly, without warning, he suddenly shoots up from his sleep, his eyes wide, and as he opens his mouth, begins coughing and spitting water up.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Fuck. Fuck you! What were those words?!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Watching, potential, learn, grow, provoke, consume. We'll go over the rest later. Come on in!

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Are we supposed to come back?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yes, you're supposed to come back in.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: We're supposed to come in!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You are.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Sorry, I found a patch of strawberries.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Everybody back?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Everybody back.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Are you okay? We're fine? Everything's good?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yeah! We're fine. We're great. Just doing dandy. Yep.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: You would tell us if something was wrong?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Of course.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: That was fast. You really fought five guys in an alleyway that fast?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Sometimes it doesn't take that long, you know what I mean?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: What does he look like? What are we doing?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: (laughter) Vasquez is a pretty good print. I think we were talking, Vasquez and Starbuck from the new Battlestar are both good character prints for Beau. Yeah, love that. As you guys bring the cart back up onto the Amber Road, northward, you begin your second-day trek. You guys push on for a few hours. Is it still Beau and Mollymauk keeping point?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Just glögg. Lots and lots of glögg.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: As you guys look off, at this conversation with the horse, the horse is currently about 30 feet off in the field, eating grass out of the field.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: If you're on the road long enough, you eat a lot of animals. You eat a lot of beasts of burden.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I wander over to the wagon and I go and sit down and start drawing, because I need to. I need to spend a little time.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I pull Nott over. Do you know what W.C. means in Zemnian?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: What does it mean?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Toilet.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: It's a pun. (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I give her a little fistbump.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: As you guys gather up, in the fresh morning air, back into the cart.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Do some pull-ups on the tree branches.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: As Beau gets nice and swole for the morning.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Vasquez.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Just a little bit.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: You're just too bad, Vasquez.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Okay. The sun does eventually close towards the Cyrios Mountains to the west and night begins to fall. At this point, you do see just over the distance in the hill as darkness comes, you can see the faint glow, a cluster of light, of a village in the distant hills. You get the sense that this is probably Alfield at the perimeter of your vision. As you continue to push forward, you've now gone past eight hours of continuous travel into the ninth hour. I would like you all to roll a constitution saving throw, please.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Let's do that.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Most of us can see in the dark, it's not a big deal.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Okay. You guys just hunker down in the wagon, okay? You be safe.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Deal.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Must be nice.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: (singing) Must be nice.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Can't say that, ah!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All righty. You guys push forward, kind of pick up the pace a bit, but the horse and those of you who are on the sides of the cart, if you wish to, you can jog along the side to alleviate some of the burden.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I'm going to scan still while I'm--

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Keeping an eye out.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: With my shitty vision.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: There you go. So you have disadvantage on ability checks until you take a long rest to get rid of it.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Natural 20.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Whoa. That's because you guys are-- oh, no, you're riding in the cart.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I'm riding.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I'm just like  What's wrong?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Nine.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Nine as well? Wow. It's like Rammstein on that side of the table. (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Nein! Cookie monster.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right. So the three of you take one level of exhaustion.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Aw. How do we do that in here?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: How does that affect us, again?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: This is a good thing to look up. Essentially, per point of exhaustion--

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: So you go to your HP.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: All right, as you go charging into the city, you're pushing past fleeing villagers and you can see some of the buildings now have been locked shut. You can see faintly through the windows furniture being boarded up from the inside. People are hunkering down for whatever this assault is. As you enter, you see the crownsguard went northward but the one creature that you saw charge in went straight towards the heart. Right now you can see there's two main streets that bisect this small village. There's one that goes at an angle, northwest to southeast. One that goes right towards the center, west to east. As you head towards the center you can see the flames are more near that central square. As you guys begin to run, push past a few more people, the smoke is starting to billow up and you can see now there's embers rising and then falling. This very faint ashy rain of small points of reddish-orange light that begin to fall with the ash. As you turn around one of the buildings, there's one of the side walls and four crownsguard all ducked down behind the wall with crossbows and longbows, all back behind it. You can see one that's adorned with more ornate armor. You see a person there with elven features, but hint at a mixed lineage. They have their helmet on the ground, showing shoulder-length blond hair that's turned red in places from a spattering of gore. Through gritted teeth they curse in pain as the bandages are applied to what you can see is a wound right around the midsection in the thigh area. Another soldier is in the process of wrapping it. You watch as they go, "Gah! You fool, bind the wound, don't stuff it." Goes and they pull a bolt back into the crossbow and nock it at the ready. Glancing up and seeing you approach, they scan past you-- most of you now with weapons at the ready, it seems, charging forward. "You!" You suddenly have an array of arrows, about five or six, come over the wall. One of them manages to pierce the back of one of the crownsguards' heads and falls and crumples to the ground. They all get lower again. You hear in the distance (growling yips). The individual looks back to you. "You, are you able-bodied?"

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Perception check.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Don't fuck this up. That's okay, 17.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: 17. In the immediate vicinity, you do not. You see more people from the village starting to run out, screaming. You see a handful of crownsguard come out, one of them looks wounded and is, "To the north. Where are they coming from?" You see the two of them dart off northward into the city. What are you guys doing?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I chase after the crownsguard.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Go after the one that stole the guy.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: We'll go after the one that stole the guy.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Is this making my spider sense tingle, can I track them?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: No.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You get the sense, at the moment, there's no active idea that these would be considered a creature that would draw your attention.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Okay, all right.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Are you leaving the cart there, on the outskirts?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yes.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: We leave the toilet behind and we run in.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: You may. I'm giving you all one round preparation before we roll initiative.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Ooh, nice.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Something clicks in your mind and you're like, "Oh, I know you."

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I'm preparing. How close am I to this thing?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: This entity would be here now. It would have just left out into that section there.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Does it see us?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: It does now.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I'm going to use Vicious Mockery at it, really quickly.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Can I invoke Duplicity?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: All right, so Beau, what are you doing? You all have one round of prep. Right now, you've gone--

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Okay, 17?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: 17 does hit.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Ooh, seven. Seven radiant damage.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Seven radiant damage, nice. As it strikes down, wham! From the creature, already watching its bones and its form shakes--

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh wait, oh wait, oh wait, I was looking at the wrong spell, hold on. Oh, 1d8 plus three! Seven plus three-- Ten!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Ten. Oh, that'll be enough. You watch as it pulls back with the Infernal shouting of Mollymauk causing it to prepare itself for a lunge. As it gets low, its jaw snapping open, you watch as bits of its skin fall onto the ground as it does so (snarl). And right before it leaps, (wham) it just smashes it into the ground and its bones go flying and scattering in every which direction.

preceding_turns.speaker: ALL
preceding_turns.text: Oh!

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Is that one of the were-thingies? Damn!

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: That ear is mine!

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Right, so you're holding back in the doorway, and you're ready to use the door to help defend you if it needs to. Okay, that ends Beau's turn. It's now their turn.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Okay, I'm going to double ba-- Ah, I can't move anymore, can I?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: No, that was your move to get there.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I'm going to spend a ki point and do Patient Defense.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Well, as your action you can just go into it without having to spend a ki point.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Oh, really?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Because you already used your bonus action. The ki point allows you to do it as a bonus action, but you can just do it as an action any time.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Ooh. Great, I'll do that.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You just go into the dodge action.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I'll just hold back and wait.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Oh that's a d4, sorry. That's six points of bludgeoning damage. Whack! As the club hits you in the back of the head as you're just climbing over the top of the wall. You have to catch yourself from tumbling face first into the dirt on the other side. You manage to catch your footing on top of the wall. But it's now up, and in combat with both you and the guard from earlier. That ends their turn. Top of the round, Beau, you're up.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Man, I hate level three.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Cool. I'm going to go pop back up to the top floor, and use my bonus action to hide.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All righty. Go ahead and make a stealth check.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Nope. Ten.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You get down low, but your hood and hair are now frizzed and poking out the side. That ends your turn, Nott. This one is going to rush forward, pursuing Jester. That one is going to attempt to continue to whale on Beau. That's going to be a 15 against Beau.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Miss.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Misses, you just duck out of the way as it tries to bite you and slam your elbow onto its snout, knocking it out of the way. Just holding on, holding off in this doorway. The one that runs up towards you is going to attempt to hit you again with a club. That is going to be a 19. Again.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Ugh!

speaker: MATT
raw_text: That ends your turn. All right. The guards go. The leader continues to run, and is now disappearing into the smoke and ash. You don't have a visual on the cart at the moment, it's now pressed into where a lot of the fire is, and it's obscuring their escape. These guards are going to move forward as they load their crossbows, and they're both-- actually, because he's on the ground laughing, you're coming out on the roof, they're going to turn and fire at the one that's in front of Molly. It does have three-quarters cover because of the wall. They're trying to help you out.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: My turn.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yep.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Can I hop around the corner again?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: (engine revving) Start up the full.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I'll go ahead and use my 30 speed to close in on the proned-out. Great, and I'll use Eldritch Blast against that.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right, it has disadvantage against it because it's prone.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: 11. As it's rolling around on the ground, it's difficult to find a proper shot, and you fire, and it hits the dirt next to it, sending a bit of dust over it. It stops laughing for a second as the dust-- He laughs even harder at the actual dust.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: That's it. That's my turn.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: That immediate first breath is filled with pain, both from the wounds you've sustained and the immediate influx of burning ash that sweeps into your lungs. Both of you come to consciousness.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I want to watch them as far as I can to try and pinpoint a direction that they're heading in.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay, make a survival check in this moment.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: It's dark, too.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: It is dark.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Not great, 11.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay. It's hard to tell with all the smoke, you can't see the moon, you can't see the placement of the sky, but you make a note of which direction they're traveling based on the street. Those who are both on the ground as Beau continues doing this, you guys begin to rush up and you see both Molly and Nott on the ground.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I run up and I'll cast Cure Wounds on Molly. Then I'm going to run over and do the same thing at level two to Nott. That's six points of healing for you, I'm sorry.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Thank you.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I'll run up and cast it on Nott. Oh no, Nott! I pull her up into my lap. Oh, that's good. 13-- oh, 13 plus five! No, plus three.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: "I have no idea. I don't know the ins and outs of their creepy bestial lifestyle. I mean, what--" And you watch as they sit down for a second, leaning against the wall, thinking and scratching at their chin, "Word came of a small hunting party of these beasts spotted to the south, so we sent a small troupe to slay them naught but two hours before they struck here. We had sent more than half of our men's strength to go and deal with them, so we were unprepared and, at the time, unguarded for the most part. We were caught at half strength. It can't be a coincidence. They must be desperate to assault head-on like this. There's plenty of small game in the surrounding wood and they're known to be provoked to violence, but I've only heard of gnolls picking off the outlying folk, those that wander too far from the fields."

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Watchmaster?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Watchmaster Bryce Feelid."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: What were those creatures? Have you encountered them before?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "We've encountered them before, yes, in smaller hunting packs, maybe a half-dozen at a time, usually on the outskirts. They don't come to civilization like this. It's generally too dangerous for them, in the smaller numbers they travel. What the fuck are they after?"

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Such a grizzly scene, they seemed to be carrying away bodies on that cart they had.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: That was weird.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Did you notice they had some undead gnolls with them?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I did.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: There were.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Have you ever seen anything like that?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "I've maybe crossed one or two as part of the roving packs, it's not terribly uncommon."

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: What turns them like that? Is there something in the water?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: I will say if you take 30 minutes to find good pieces of charcoal, and roll an investigation check.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Well, I would normally put you up in the Candleglow Inn but--" They gesture over towards the large building that you had walked through that's now currently--

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: It's literally glowing like a candle right now.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "That is ironic now that you point it out."

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I feel really bad for saying that out loud.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Let's just not let Thadeus know that his life is--"

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I have a question. After listening to this for a while, I start looking around the town, would you say that with all these burnt buildings everywhere, that if I start picking through the rubble I would find enough charcoal to reduce the costs I would need for Find Familiar, eventually? Because I still need the incense and the herbs, but if I get a lot of charcoal from these burnt homes--

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Bryce says, "We hope to hear back in the morning of our scouts' findings. If you wish to, if you can hunt whatever remains of these creatures and bring back proof that they've been wiped out, I'll see what I can have sent southward of Zadash to make it worth your while."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Crute?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Tell him that Bryce sent you and you are being put up by the Crown for as long as you are hunting these creatures."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: We appreciate that. Hopefully, there won't be another call to action while they're here, but you can count on us if there is.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Of course. Well." They reach into a pouch and begin to count out the gold for the number of ears you've collected. With twelve ears--

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: 360 gold.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Mm-hmm.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Dope.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: After bringing the ears forward, they glance over. "Actually, I'll be right back. I may need to acquire a bit more change." And after a brief wait, Bryce returns and you are paid the full amount.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Damn.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: So 360 gold at your disposal.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Should we divvy it up equally?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: If we do, that's 60 gold apiece. That's Laura talking. (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I'm fine with that.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Barn. Broad Barn. They point it out to you, it's on the western side of the town. "There, we'll make sure you get the basic supplies necessary. Hopefully, we'll have information on where to go, and if you so choose, we could conscribe you to be under our intent to destroy these creatures. Think on it, rest, we'll speak in the morning." Bryce turns around and limps off with a few of the other crownsguard. As you guys eventually find your way to the Feed and Mead, you are immediately met by Crute, who is this ornery, salty-looking dwarf of tousled white-gray hair, this single tuft of a beard that dangles down towards the belly button before it hits a ring of gold, goes, "Oh, so I've been told you're the ones who were coming to sleep. Got one of our two rooms for you."

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: In the meantime, you wouldn't have anything that might help us and our little expedition, would you? Healing potion might be nice if you got one hiding away somewhere in a closet, forgotten.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "I don't mean to be brusque, but at the moment I think we have a number of our guard who are in more desperate need."

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Fair.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I can help them... in the morning. (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "I'm sure that would be appreciated. For the moment, you all look about as battered as I feel. Rest up, come--" Let's see, they would be at-- "Meet me outside of the Broad Barn."

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: The "Rod Bawn"?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "The Broad Barn."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: The broad?
